title: The Mirror of Literature, Amusement, and Instruction. Volume 17, No. 492, June 4, 1831

Bramber is noted for its castle ruins, highlighting its historical past in the context of its current desolate state. The text provides fascinating insights into the nature of local governance and electoral practices in these boroughs, revealing a humorous yet critical view of past political integrity and illustrating the impact of geography on these communities.
